item.title: DDA review is a right call says ACT NOW!
item.description: [Act Now PNG] There is clearly a huge transparency and accountability gap that still exists within DDAs. The call by the Constitutional Law Reform Commission to review DDA law is a right call.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: How a local fintech solved two ‘serious’ problems with land management
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Port Moresby firm NiuPay has found a way to turn Papua New Guinea's new strata title law into a working, digital reality. Its Digital Strata Title Platform was one of the winners at this year's Innovation PNG Awards, with founder James Inglis sharing how it works – and the problems it solves – in hi

item.title: USP Research Helps Deliver Tuvalu’s First Waste Plastic-to-Fuel Project
item.description: [Education News PNG] Tuvalu has taken a major step towards transforming plastic waste into a valuable source of energy with the launch of the country’s first plastic waste The post USP Research Helps Deliver Tuvalu’s First Waste Plastic-to-Fuel Project first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: PNGDF Integrates with Australian Forces for Wantok Warrior
item.description: [Inside PNG] Colonel Aruma witnessed history as PNGDF mortars roared on Australian soil, marking a major milestone for the bilateral defense partnership. The post PNGDF Integrates with Australian Forces for Wantok Warrior first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNGDF Integrates with Australian Forces for Wantok

item.title: NSW Blues Complete State of Origin Series Sweep with Dominant Game 3 Victory
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] The New South Wales Blues produced a commanding performance to defeat the Queensland Maroons 30-12 in Game 3 of the Ampol State of Origin series at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane on Wednesday. Played in fine weather on good ground conditions, the Blues showed their class from start to finish to close o

item.title: Papua New Guinea and The National Identity Card
item.description: [Pawa TV] The National Identity Card project in Papua New Guinea, launched in 2014, aims to enhance citizen registration and service delivery. It integrates with the PNG Civil and Identity Registry to streamline operations and improve access nationwide. The post Papua New Guinea and The National Identity Card

item.title: The Coolest Vanity Apps for You and Your Girls
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG's P'nyang Gas Project to Get Road Boost From Ok Tedi's K30 Million Pledge
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG's P'nyang Gas Project to Get Road Boost From Ok Tedi's K30 Million Pledge/Photo supplied A K30 million commitment from Ok Tedi Mining Limited will help fund a new road connecting to the P'nyang Project, widely seen as Papua New Guinea's next significant gas development, the company has announced

item.title: PC Podcast Episode 20: PNG’s London Broncos Trio
item.description: [Post Courier] In a milestone 20th episode of the NASFUND PC Podcast Series, we sat down for an exclusive chat with the history-making Papua New Guineans currently lighting up English rugby league with the London Broncos. The post PC Podcast Episode 20: PNG’s London Broncos Trio appeared first on Post Courier .

item.title: AMBULANCE OFFICERS GRADUATE, READY TO SERVE
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GEORGINA MICHAEL Twenty-six officers from the National St John Ambulance (NStJA) graduated today, marking an important milestone in strengthening Papua New Guinea’s pre-hospital emergency care workforce. The Ambulance Officer Level 1 graduation was officiated by the Governor-General of Papua New

item.title: Heavy Rain, Flood risk warning issued for several PNG Provinces
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Papua New Guinea National Weather Service (PNG NWS) last night issued a Heavy Rain and Potential Flood Warning for several parts of the country. It warned that intense rainfall over the next 12 to 24 hours could trigger flash flooding, river flooding and landslides. Related news: https://pnghaus

item.title: Ol ami rausim planti bom long East New Britain provins
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol bom ekspet na sapot opisa bilong dispela wok i kam long Australia, PNG, New Zealand, Canada, France, Japan, UK na USA i bin stap insait long wanpela operesen we ol i rausim inap 2,200 bom bilong Wol Woa 2 long East New Britain provins long Jun dispela yia. Australia i go pas long dispela wok anin

item.title: Mining Project Sparks Fears of Environmental Disaster
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Major mining project that could discharge hundreds of millions of tonnes of waste into the sea off Papua New Guinea

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘We’re always looking for opportunities’: Air Niugini’s new CEO on his first items of business
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Alan Milne was appointed to his second term as CEO of Air Niugini in early 2026, following a previous stint in 2018-2020. In this interview with Business Advantage PNG , Milne speaks about changing market conditions and plans for more planes and new routes, as well as a pending new corporate strateg

item.title: Protecting Pacific Taro
item.description: [Education News PNG] USP Researcher Tackles Climate Challenges As the world marks World Rural Development Day on 6 July, attention turns to the vital role rural communities play The post Protecting Pacific Taro first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Air Niugini Returns to Japan
item.description: [Inside PNG] Air Niugini resumes direct Port Moresby-Tokyo flights on July 18, halving travel times and boosting trade, tourism, and regional connectivity. The post Air Niugini Returns to Japan first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Air Niugini Returns to Japan appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: PNG Leader Urges World Powers to Keep Pacific "Ocean of Peace" After Chinese Missile Test
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has urged major military powers across the globe to honour the Pacific as a zone of peace, after China fired a long-range ballistic missile into open waters in the South Pacific region as part of a test launch, directly affecting waters close to Papua New Guinea and its n

item.title: Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea faces significant gold smuggling issues, undermining its economy and reducing revenue for essential services. Efforts are being made to enhance monitoring and disrupt smuggling networks for better resource management. The post Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAW

item.title: Queensland University Teams Up With PNG Government on Digital Push
item.description: [PNG Facts] A new partnership between Papua New Guinea and an Australian university is set to shape how the country builds its digital government systems in the years ahead, following the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding with the Queensland University of Technology. The MoU, finalised under the Marape-R

item.title: New Ambulance Officers told to stay committed
item.description: [Post Courier] Twenty-six (26) new ambulance officers graduated today from the St. John Ambulance program, with leaders urging them to stay dedicated to saving lives and to pursue further training. item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI The Gulf Provincial Government (GPG) has welcomed the Papua LNG Development Forum initiative and affirms its commitment to work cooperatively with the National Government, the National Petroleum Authority (NPA), development partners, Central Province and customary landowners. Speaking

item.title: PM Marape: PNG-Australia ties focus on People and Prosperity
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape says Papua New Guinea’s relationship with Australia has evolved into an equal partnership focused on creating more opportunities for people, businesses and both countries. Speaking after the annual Papua New Guinea–Australia Leaders’ Dialogue this week. PM Marape said the

item.title: Mt. Bagana na El Niño givim hevi long Bogenvil
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Maunten paia long Mt. Bagana wantaim drai bilong El Nino i kamapim planti hevi long ol manmeri long Bogenvil. Ol i sot long gutpela wara na kaikai long ples we i stap klostu long we asis bilong maunten i pundaun.

item.description: [Act Now PNG] Constitutional Law Reform Commission (CLRC) will review the laws on DDA

item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Renovations and upgrades are well underway across Steamships Trading Company’s extensive hospitality portfolio, which includes Papua New Guinea’s largest hotel chain, Coral Sea Hotels. The new head of its hospitality division reveals the key changes and explains what’s driving the investment. The po

item.title: PPL to Appeal Court Ordered Payment
item.description: [Inside PNG] PPL will appeal a major court-ordered payment. Executives cite severe legal errors, vowing a fierce challenge to protect company assets. The post PPL to Appeal Court Ordered Payment first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PPL to Appeal Court Ordered Payment appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: Newmont PNG expands trade training with national accreditation
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Newmont PNG has reached another milestone in workforce development after its Lihir Technical Training Centre secured national accreditation to conduct onsite Belt Splicing trade testing, giving more Papua New Guineans access to recognised trade qualifications without leaving the mine site. Newmont P

item.title: TVET and Universities in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's education system offers two main pathways: Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) focuses on practical skills for immediate employment, while universities emphasise theoretical knowledge and long-term career preparation. The post TVET and Universities in Papua New

item.title: PNG Air Niugini Restarts Direct Port Moresby–Tokyo Flights After Six-Year Break
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's national airline will once again connect PNG directly with Japan, with Air Niugini confirming that nonstop services between Port Moresby and Tokyo will resume from July 18, 2026. The return of the route restores a key international link that has been absent since 2020 and is expec

item.title: USA CELEBRATES 250 YEARS OF INDEPENDENCE
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GEORGINA MICHAEL The United States of America celebrated its 250th Independence anniversary at the U.S. Embassy in Port Moresby yesterday. The ceremony was officiated by Prime Minister James Marape, U.S. Chargé d’Affaires Mr. Alex Berenberg and senior citizens and representative of business house

item.title: K239.8 million Goroka Road upgrade to 4-Lane signed
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Governor-General Sir Bob Dadae yesterday, executed a K239.8 million contract on behalf of the State for the upgrade of the Goroka section of the Highlands Highway into a four-lane road. The project will be fully funded under the Government’s Connect PNG Programme. The contract, valued at K239,799,29

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: PNG and the Failure of EUDR Benchmarking: When “Low Risk” Ignores Reality
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Papua New Guinea’s designation as “low risk” in the EUDR may now be one of the clearest examples of the disconnect with reality.

item.title: PNG and Australia Enter Historic Pukpuk Alliance Treaty
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea and Australia have officially become formal treaty allies after the Pukpuk Treaty came into legal effect today, marking a new chapter in relations between the two neighbouring countries. The agreement became legally binding following the exchange of instruments of ratification, comp

item.title: UPNG Overhauls curriculum to secure High-Paying Jobs for graduates
item.description: [Post Courier] "Your degree will get you the job, but your soft skills will get you the promotion." The University of Papua New Guinea (UPNG) has launched an aggressive, institution-wide curriculum review to ensure its graduates secure top-tier employment and command the highest salaries in a rapidly changing glob

item.title: Dei District Signs DCDC agreement with DFCDR

The agreement was signed yesterday between member for Dei district Hon. Desmond Kipa and Minister for Community Devel

item.title: Active City Development Program, Marie Stopes PNG promote healthy living

The workshop is aimed at encouraging the city residents to adopt healthier lifestyles and take greater responsibi

item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: Pukpuk Treaty: PNG and Australia Officially Become Allies
item.description: [Inside PNG] PAPUA NEW GUINEA and Australia are now, formally allies with the, entry into force of the Pukpuk Treaty. This means the articles outlined in the Bilateral Security Deal are now legally binding and can be implemented. The announcement made on 11th July by both Prime Ministers will strengthen the inte

item.title: PNG names Dr Nicole Haley to lead Electoral Commission
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has announced Dr Nicole Haley as Papua New Guinea's new Electoral Commissioner, entrusting the election specialist with overseeing preparations for the country's 2027 National General Election. The appointment follows weeks of speculation over the position and comes after

item.title: Women in PNG Politics

The Blues outplayed the Maroons across both halves, scoring five tries to thre

item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By ESWIN PLESIO Central Province Governor Rufina Peter has called for fairness, transparency and the inclusion of all legitimate landowners as discussions on the Papua LNG Project officially got underway at the Papua LNG Development Forum in Port Moresby today. Speaking at the forum’s opening at APE

item.title: Ok Tedi Mine Limited commits K30 million to P’nyang Road
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Ok Tedi Mining Limited (OTML) has committed K30 million towards the construction of the Sisimakam–P’nyang Road. The company said in a media statement today that the project is a major infrastructure project that will provide road access to the proposed P’nyang LNG Project in Western Province. Re

item.title: Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji
item.description: [Pawa TV] Fiji significantly leads in tourism development over Papua New Guinea, generating USD 2.5 billion compared to PNG's USD 182.94 million, highlighting the need for PNG to improve accessibility and marketing. item.description: [PNG Facts] Regional air travel in the southwest Pacific has received a fresh boost with Solomon Airlines reinstating direct passenger services between Honiara and Port Moresby after an absence of 15 years, restoring one of Melanesia's key aviation links. The relaunch coincided with celebrations marking the Sol

item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A small poultry farm in Papua New Guinea’s Central Province has found a sustainable way of reducing its dependence on expensive stock feed. item.title: Early Campaigning in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Concerns grow in Papua New Guinea over early campaigning, which breaches electoral laws. item.title: PNG congratulates Solomon Islands on 48th Independence
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has congratulated the Government and people of the Solomon Islands on the country’s 48th Independence Anniversary, reaffirming Papua New Guinea’s commitment to strengthening its longstanding Melanesian partnership with its Pacific neighbor. item.title: Dei bai-ileksen suruk bek tupela de
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] PNGEC i wari long hevi bilong sekyuriti na i surukim bai-ileksen bilong Dei Open i go bek tupela de

item.title: ACT NOW! files second legal challenge against the PNG Forest Authority
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Second legal action against the PNG Forest Authority filed by ACT NOW

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘The skills that matter are changing fast’: Q&A with Ben Hamer, keynote speaker at 2026 PNG Investment Conference
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Dr Ben Hamer, an international expert on the future of work, shares his views on which skills will be most in demand in the economies of the future – and where Papua New Guinea fits into the changing nature of work. The post ‘The skills that matter are changing fast’: Q&A with Ben Hamer, keynote spe

item.title: JICA and PNG Break Ground on Major Fisheries College Upgrade in Kavieng
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Government of Japan, through the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA), has joined the Government of Papua New Guinea and the National Fisheries Authority to The post JICA and PNG Break Ground on Major Fisheries College Upgrade in Kavieng first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: AI on the sly: more Aussies are using chatbots for work
item.description: [Inside PNG] Australian employees are turning to artificial intelligence or AI tools to get ahead at work without the knowledge or permission of their employers. The post AI on the sly: more Aussies are using chatbots for work first appeared on Inside PNG . The post AI on the sly: more Aussies are using chatbots

item.title: PNG Aviation Network to Benefit from New Niusky Investments and Leadership
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea's air traffic management system is poised for further improvement as Niusky Pacific Limited advances plans to modernise its national aviation infrastructure under a newly appointed board of directors. With fresh leadership now in place, the state-owned air navigation service provide

item.title: Cancer Treatment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Cancer poses a significant health challenge in Papua New Guinea, exacerbated by late diagnoses, limited screening, and inadequate treatment facilities. Strengthening oncology services and prevention efforts is essential to improve outcomes. The post Cancer Treatment in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG PM Marape Removes Minister Win Daki from Commerce Portfolio Pending SME Fund Probe
item.description: [PNG Facts] Prime Minister James Marape has stripped Minister Win Daki of the Commerce and Industry portfolio as the Government launches an independent investigation into allegations involving the administration of Papua New Guinea's Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Fund. The move also requires Commerce and In

item.title: UPNG launches STEM pathway and specialised Teacher Training to drive PNG’s Digital Rebirth
item.description: [Post Courier] The University of Papua New Guinea (UPNG) has announced a major expansion of its academic initiatives for 2027, launching a university preparatory pathway for Grade 12 students alongside a new postgraduate program designed to retrain secondary school teachers in technology. To access this post, you

item.title: Office of Censorship host Media Information session
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GEORGINA MICHAEL THE Office of Censorship held a Media information session with media personnels from the mainstream media yesterday. The workshop was held under the theme “Clarifying the Roles and Functions of the Office of Censorship and the National Censorship Board.” The workshop aimed at imp

item.title: Solomon Airlines resumes Port Moresby-Honiara flights after 15-year break
item.description: [The PNG Sun] As Solomon Islands is celebrating its 48th Independence anniversary, the Solomon Airlines has resumed direct flights between Port Moresby and Honiara after a 15-year hiatus. This direct flight has restored weekly air services aimed at boosting tourism, trade, business, people-to-people and regional

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: ACT NOW! files second legal challenge against PNG Forest Authority
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! has filed a second legal action against the PNG Forest Authority

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Iconic brewer looking to make a social impact in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] SP Brewery’s beers have been sold in PNG since 1952, almost a quarter-century before independence. Now the company is looking to invest in social change alongside business growth opportunities, its Managing Director Ed Weggemans tells Business Advantage PNG . The post Iconic brewer looking to make a

item.title: PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae
item.description: [Education News PNG] Thirty-one cadets from the PNG Joint Forces Academy successfully completed a major field training exercise in Lae this week as part of their final military The post PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery
item.description: [Inside PNG] NAQIA restricts Australian poultry imports after H5N1 bird flu found in WA wild birds. Free-range WA products suspended whilst barn-raised are safe. The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imp

item.title: PNG PM extends congratulations to US on 250 years of independence
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has sent his congratulations to United States President Donald J. Trump, his administration and the American people following celebrations marking 250 years of American independence on July 4. The Prime Minister described the milestone as a significant mo

item.title: Nasfund start rollout of mobile services booths
item.description: [Post Courier] Nasfund is the first in the superannuation arena to take the full Nasfund office to its members' location. Chief Executive Officer Rajeev Sharma stated that most of the time, many members who turn up at the Nasfund branch usually go to check their balance, get their statements, get a membership iden

item.title: Exercise Wantok Warrior deepens PNG-Australia Defence ties
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PAPUA New Guinea Defence Force (PNGDF) soldiers have returned from Australia after completing the first phase of Exercise Wantok Warrior 2026-a joint military exercise aimed at strengthening defence cooperation between Papua New Guinea and Australia. The exercise was held in Townsville, Queensland,

item.title: TSC advertises 42, 000 teaching positions for 2027 school year
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Teaching Service Commission (TSC) has advertised 42,000 teaching positions nationwide for the 2027 academic year and directed eligible teachers and graduating education students to submit applications by July 31. Acting TSC Chairman Maini Mike Ugaia announced yesterday in a statement that the va

item.title: More complaints of illegal FCA logging
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Communities in remote Nakanai region of West New Britain say their forests are being illegally destroyed by a Malaysian logging company

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Executive appointments at NBC, board appointment at Digicel Pacific, board appointment at Santos. The post People Moves: National Banking Corporation, Digicel Pacific, Santos appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: Ten (10) Arrested Including Two (2) Chinese Nationals Over Alleged Murder Of Young PNG Man
item.description: [Inside PNG] Police in Port Moresby have arrested ten suspects over the death of a 20 year old man and the attempted murder of another victim. Among the arrests were four security guards from Fix Security Firm, four shop assistants and two Chinese expatriates from Cash and Carry Shopping Center at Gerehu’s Rainb

item.title: Reshuffle looms for PNG Government as Marape tightens performance checks
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's Cabinet lineup is set for an overhaul, with Prime Minister James Marape signalling that a reshuffle will be announced within days. Speaking ahead of the expected Thursday or Friday announcement, the Prime Minister said the move stems from a lengthy review of how well ministers hav

item.title: 48 Kerowagi farmers urged to start farming commercial crops during 18th graduation
item.description: [Post Courier] Forty-eight farmers in Mingende, Kerowagi District, Chimbu Province, have graduated with over K30,000 worth of potato seeds and farming materials to launch their agricultural ventures. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Month , Web – Three-Month , W

item.title: OPS presents annual report, recommends increased funding
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI THE Office of the Public Solicitor (OPS) has presented its 2025 Annual Report to the Governor-General, detailing its operations and recommending improvements to strengthen the office’s services. Public Solicitor Leslie Mamu presented the report and emphasised that one of the key recom

item.title: Solomon Airlines launches special airfare sale with new Port Moresby-Honiara service; one-way K980, return K2030
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Solomon Airlines has launched a special international airfare sale to mark the commencement of its new Port Moresby-Honiara service and the Solomon Islands’ 48th Independence anniversary, offering discounted fares for travel between the two Melanesian nations. The promotion was announced on Monday a

item.title: Laundering cases in PNG enforce historic round log export ban
item.description: [Act Now PNG] An international financial crackdown is underway in PNG with global intelligence agency INTERPOL

item.title: Hunters Hunted Down: Seagulls Maul PNG Hunters in 54-0 Thrashing
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Hunters were thumped 54-0 by Wynnum Manly Seagulls in a Round 16 clash played at BMD Kougari Oval in Brisbane on Sunday, with the home side crossing for ten tries in a comprehensive victory over the visitors. Hunters Hunted Down: Seagulls Maul PNG in 54-0 Thrashing The Seagulls set the tone earl

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: PNG PM UNVEILS K316 MILLION INFRASTRUCTURE DRIVE FOR GULF
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has rolled out a K316 million infrastructure package targeting roads, bridges and community facilities for rural Gulf Province residents, part of a broader push to reach the majority of citizens living outside urban centres. Standing before villagers gath
